End of 2026 PREDICTION:

Bitcoin 31 Dec 2026:
$190,000 - $210,000 🚀

How did I arrive at this prediction?

I took some of the biggest predictions out there and cleaned them up instead of just averaging them blindly.

These are the recent public predictions:

$126,000 — CitiGroup
$148,000 — Pantera Capital
$150,000 — Standard Chartered
$170,000 — JPMorgan
$180,000 — VanEck
$189,000 — Tom Lee
$200,000 — Standard Chartered
$250,000 — Tim Draper
$275,000 — Cathie Wood
$350,000 — Robert Kiyosaki
$400,000 — Anthony Scaramucci
$500,000 — Chamath Palihapitiya
$500,000 — Mike Novogratz

First, I split them into three groups. Big banks like Citi, JPMorgan, and Standard Chartered got more weight because they tend to adjust their forecasts when they’re wrong. Then I put crypto funds like Pantera and VanEck in the middle. Finally, I grouped the super bullish names who often overshoot and rarely walk back their calls.

From there, I assigned weights. Tier 1 (banks) got a 1.5x weighting, Tier 2 (crypto funds) got 1.0x, and Tier 3 (high-variance forecasters) got 0.5x. This creates a bias toward forecasts with historically lower error margins.

Then I adjusted the numbers.

For the most aggressive predictions, I applied a 20 percent downward normalization to correct for systematic upside bias. This reduces the impact of extreme outliers in the distribution and tightens the variance.

At that point, I calculated a weighted mean across all tiers. The effective contribution came out roughly as follows: 42 percent from Tier 1, 30 percent from Tier 2, and 28 percent from Tier 3 after penalties.

The weighted average converged around $188k, with a trimmed range between $180k and $200k once outliers were suppressed.

After combining everything, the result comes out close to $190k.

What’s interesting is this lands right in the middle of the realistic range, not the hype. It shows how much extreme predictions can distort the picture if you don’t filter them properly 📊

So while you see $400k and $500k calls everywhere, the smarter money is quietly pointing much lower, but still very bullish 📈

The Bitcoin market just broke.

Not price.

Supply.

And what just snapped this week…

means $10 million Bitcoin isn’t a prediction.

It’s a consequence.

Not eventually.

Mechanically.

Everything you’re watching right now…

is still focused on price.

Charts. Levels. Narratives.

But the real shift?

Is happening underneath the market.

Because while price looks calm…

availability is collapsing.

And almost no one is fully processing it.

Because they’re still watching price… not availability.

Here’s what just broke…

$9 trillion Charles Schwab just rolled out Bitcoin trading internally.

Employees are already trading it.

Client access is launching in weeks.

That’s not speculation.

That’s infrastructure.

At the same time…

Morgan Stanley - a $10 trillion giant - just revealed only 1 in 5 clients own Bitcoin.

Which means 80%… still haven’t entered.

And now 16,000 of their advisors are actively selling Bitcoin ETFs.

$100 million flowed into a single product in its first week.

This isn’t saturation. It’s ignition.

Now layer in what’s happening behind the scenes.

BlackRock just extended its buying streak to 7 straight days.

$1.65 billion absorbed in a single week.

Yesterday alone:

1,009 Bitcoin.

Gone. In a single day.

Let that sink in.

And this is happening while something even more important is unfolding.

Miners have stopped selling.

Since February.

At the exact same time…

whales accumulated over 270,000 Bitcoin in just 30 days.

One of the largest accumulation events in history.

Read that again.

So think about what that means.

The two natural sources of supply in this market:

👉 miners
👉 large holders

Both are now locking up coins.

Not releasing them.

Meanwhile…

exchange reserves have dropped to just 2.67 million Bitcoin.

But here’s the part most people miss:

👉 Only ~1.9 million of that is actually liquid.

The rest?

Locked. Illiquid. Not moving.

That’s the real supply.

It’s disappearing.

The Bitcoin that can actually be bought.

And it’s getting thinner…

from both sides.

Now zoom out one level further.

Network activity just hit an 8-year low.

Short-term traders?

Gone.

What’s left?

Long-term holders.

Quietly accumulating.

Not trading.

Not reacting.

Just removing supply from circulation.

This is what early-stage absorption looks like.

And it’s happening while global demand is expanding.

Relentlessly.

Michael Saylor just said it plainly:

“We are making $500 million a day with Bitcoin.”

And added:

“We may very well be the most profitable company in the U.S. growing the fastest right now.”

That’s not a company.

That’s a machine.

A capital engine.

Converting dollars into Bitcoin every single day.

And it doesn’t stop.

Because this isn’t about short-term price anymore.

As Jack Mallers put it:

“Anyone who understands Bitcoin… it becomes their entire portfolio.”

Not 1%.

Not a hedge.

The whole thing.

That’s the mindset shift happening right now.

And it changes everything.

Because when capital starts thinking like that…

allocation doesn’t trickle in.

It floods in.

Now bring it back to the only number that matters.

Bitcoin produces:

👉 450 BTC per day

That’s it.

Fixed supply.

Now compare that to demand.

BlackRock: billions per week
Corporations: accumulating aggressively
Advisors: onboarding clients
Sovereigns: beginning to enter

And 80% of traditional capital?

Still not allocated.

There is no scenario where that balances.

There is no release valve.

Now add one more layer.

Look at what Bitcoin used to buy.

iPhone 4S:
👉 47.9 BTC

iPhone 5:
👉 15.9 BTC

iPhone 6:
👉 0.5 BTC

Let that sink in.

From 47 Bitcoin… to half a coin.

Fast forward to today:

iPhone 16:
👉 0.013 BTC

iPhone 17:
👉 0.007 BTC

Read that again.

From 47 BTC… to less than 0.01.

Every cycle, Bitcoin buys more.

Because supply gets tighter.

And price doesn’t adjust slowly.

It reprices.

This is where the shift becomes unavoidable.

Because markets don’t move based on narratives.

They move based on constraints.

And right now…

Bitcoin is hitting a constraint.

Available supply.

Because once enough Bitcoin gets locked away…

price doesn’t gradually rise.

It gaps.

$100K breaks
$250K accelerates
$500K triggers competition
$1M changes the system

But beyond that?

There are no clear levels.

Only higher bids.

Because there isn’t enough supply in between.

This is why $5M Bitcoin isn’t extreme.

It’s mechanical.

And $10M?

That’s what happens next. Period.

When global capital collides with a market that cannot expand.

Because Bitcoin doesn’t adjust to demand.

Demand adjusts to Bitcoin.

And that’s what just broke.

So the question isn’t:

Will Bitcoin reach $1M?

The real question is:

What happens when the world realizes…

there isn’t enough Bitcoin left…

for everyone trying to buy it?

Because when that realization hits…

price doesn’t rise.

It gaps.

And it reprices everything.

My Prediction: The new Bitcoin Movie (previously named Killing Satoshi) will find it difficult to make it to a Cinema or Streaming Platform.

Let me explain why:

It will be shown first in Cannes, next month, this happens so a distributor or streaming service makes an offer for it.

But the official synopsis itself shows why it will attract trouble for any distributor: 

"A high-stakes conspiracy thriller that asks the question no one in power wants answered: Why have the world’s most powerful and wealthiest people, Jack Dorsey, Mark Zuckerberg, Visa and virtually every major crypto organization,  poured hundreds of millions of dollars into a single mega-organization called COPA, with one sole purpose: to destroy one man?"

No company will touch such a story, if they really mention these people in the movie and allege they were behind COPA and that they tried to destroy "Satoshi".

This would probably cost the distributors Millions after being dragged into endless court battles for tarnishing their names without any proof.

There is no real life connection between these Billionaires, visa and COPA.

Unless there is no new proof showing this connection (I highly doubt it) then this movie will be a flop.

Naming real billionaires and companies while implying a massive conspiracy to "destroy one man" (without strong on-screen proof) could invite defamation, libel, or false light claims, especially from Dorsey, Meta, Visa, or crypto firms. Even if the film calls itself "fiction" or "inspired by," the specific naming + accusatory tone raises the odds of lawsuits. Distributors and streamers hate expensive legal headaches that could drag on for years and cost millions in defense/settlements. 

Hollywood has pulled or altered projects for far less.

I am really sorry for the actors especially Gal Gadot and Casey Affleck as they probably signed up for this without knowing anything about the real story and I really enjoy their acting, they are actually amongst the top 10 actors for me.

I think the only way this movie can make it is if somehow the synopsis is amended and names in the movie of the billionaires or companies are changed completely.

I also think the fact they already changed the name from Killing Satoshi to Bitcoin shows they are already trying to tone it down.

I would have gone to see the movie even if it portrayed someone who isn't Satoshi as Satoshi but I doubt it will make it to any cinema near me now after this synopsis was released.

KALSHI PLANS US CRYPTO PERPETUAL FUTURES LAUNCH, TAKING ON COINBASE AND ROBINHOOD

@Kalshi is preparing to launch crypto perpetual futures in the US, starting with Bitcoin-linked contracts, according to a report from The Information. The move leverages the prediction market platform's existing CFTC Designated Contract Market status and its recent approval to offer margin trading, positioning it for direct competition with Coinbase, Robinhood, and offshore venues that have historically captured US perps demand.

The timing lines up with CFTC Chairman Michael Selig's March signal that the agency intends to permit "true perpetual futures" for digital assets in the US, a major shift from the long-standing offshore-only status of the product. $BTC and $ETH perpetual futures volumes alone grew from $1 trillion in 2019 to $42 trillion by 2025, underscoring the addressable market Kalshi is aiming at.

Kalshi has been teasing a "Timeless" product reveal for April 27 in New York, with a torus-themed launch video from CEO Tarek Mansour that most observers read as a perpetuals signal. The platform now holds 89% of US prediction market volume per a Bank of America report and was recently valued at $22 billion following a Coatue-led round.

Not a prediction.

Something just changed in the structure of the Bitcoin market.

The mechanism behind it.

Almost nobody sees it yet.

Not even close.

Because what most people are still seeing as “demand”…

Is actually turning into a machine.

And that machine is scaling.

It’s already happening in real time.

Michael Saylor just updated his tracker again.

766,970 BTC accumulated.

Another acquisition follows.

Like clockwork.

The machine isn’t coming.

Michael Saylor isn’t just buying Bitcoin.

At scale.

Forever.

It doesn’t.

Because markets don’t move linearly.

Bitcoin has something most assets don’t:

A multiplier effect.

When new capital enters Bitcoin…

It doesn’t just increase market cap one-for-one.

It reprices the entire asset.

It only takes about $1.8 trillion of net inflows to push Bitcoin to $1 million.

At 5x:

Roughly $3.7 trillion.

About $6 trillion.

This system only needs to capture around 0.5% to 1.5% of that market.

Not 10%.

One percent.

0.0018%.

That’s not adoption.
That’s a prototype.

Calling it one of the most undervalued companies in the world.

Not because of earnings.

Because of Bitcoin.

Because this isn’t just about buying Bitcoin.

It’s about transforming Bitcoin into a global fixed-income asset.

High yield.
High liquidity.

Scarce collateral.

There is nothing else like it in the financial system.

Even Coinbase’s CEO just said it out loud:

And he’s not talking to retail.

He’s talking to capital allocators.

Funds.
Institutions.
Corporate treasuries.
Sovereign capital.

That’s how markets work.

Which means this isn’t just a Bitcoin story anymore.

This is a capital markets story.

Because if STRC - or products like it - start absorbing even a fraction of global debt flows…

Bitcoin doesn’t just go up.

It gets repriced.

Violently.

And this is where it breaks most people’s understanding of markets.

This system is reflexive.

The more Bitcoin gets absorbed…

The tighter the available supply becomes.

The tighter supply becomes…

The stronger the price reaction to new capital.

The stronger the price reaction…

The more attractive the yield and collateral profile becomes.

Which pulls in more capital.
Which absorbs more supply.
Which drives price higher.

And the cycle feeds itself.

This is not a normal market dynamic.
This is a feedback loop.

And most people are still looking at it like it’s just another cycle.

They’re watching price.

The infrastructure behind Bitcoin is being rebuilt in real time.

Wall Street is integrating it.

Barclays.

Trillion-dollar banks are now openly moving toward Bitcoin integration.

Corporations are accumulating it.

At scale.
Consistently.
Relentlessly.

Not really.

This is no longer about whether Bitcoin reaches $5M.

Because once it reaches escape velocity…

There is no off switch.

No supply response.

No way to slow it down.

New capital starts competing for an asset with vanishing float…

It will gap.

It’s the creation of a system that turns demand into a continuous force.

There is no ceiling.

Only supply constraints.

They’re disappearing faster than anyone realizes.

It’s happening faster than people can react.

So the question isn’t whether Bitcoin reaches $5M.

It’s how much is left…

When the machine finishes absorbing the rest.

Coinbase & Robinhood Turn to Prediction Markets for Growth

Facing weak earnings, Coinbase and Robinhood are betting on prediction markets as their next growth driver.

With Bitcoin (-23%) and Ethereum (-29%) declines, trading volumes dropped significantly — Coinbase alone fell from $66B to $54B.

To reduce reliance on volatile trading fees, both firms are expanding into prediction markets, tokenization, and private market access.

Prediction markets are particularly strategic: they transform trading into information, positioning platforms as probability-driven financial infrastructure rather than simple exchanges.

However, regulatory risks remain. New York authorities have already filed lawsuits, framing prediction markets as gambling, intensifying the legal battle over classification in the U.S.

The shift is clear:
“Exchange → Information Market → Financial Infrastructure.”
The platforms that lead this transition are likely to dominate the next cycle.

🚀Crypto Pulse | April 25, 2026🚀

Crypto today is being shaped by large whale positioning, stablecoin enforcement, prediction market regulation, and new Bitcoin fork debate.

A Hyperliquid whale is reportedly holding a $38M short against Bitcoin, alongside short exposure to several altcoins.
That does not automatically mean the market must dump, but it shows how visible onchain derivatives positioning can now influence sentiment.

US authorities also froze $344M in crypto linked to Iran, one day after Tether reportedly froze the same amount in USDT following a law enforcement request.
This is a major reminder that stablecoins are not neutral infrastructure when sanctions, enforcement, and issuer controls are involved.

Prediction markets are facing more legal pressure.
The CFTC has added New York to the list of states it is suing over attempts to restrict prediction market activity under state gambling laws.
The bigger fight is about who controls these markets: federal derivatives regulators or state gaming authorities.

Bitcoin developer Paul Sztorc also announced a proposed BTC hard fork called eCash, expected to introduce a competing layer-1 chain and several layer-2 scaling networks.
This does not mean Bitcoin itself is changing immediately, but it shows that scaling debates and fork experiments are still alive.

The TRUMP memecoin story also stayed in the headlines, with top holders expected to attend a Trump luncheon.
That is less of a market structure signal and more of a reminder that memecoins are increasingly being used for access, attention, and political branding.

Today’s market signal:

• Whale shorts are becoming visible market narratives
• Stablecoin freezes show the power of issuer-level control
• Prediction markets are entering a federal vs state regulation fight
• Bitcoin fork debates are resurfacing around scaling and competing systems

Takeaway:
Watch large onchain positions, but do not blindly follow them.
Watch who can freeze assets, not just who holds them.
Watch where regulation is trying to define the rules before the market grows bigger.

That is where the real signal is.

Check first. Then decide.

Daily Market Report #713

Thursday. Almost the end of the week. Markets feel relatively calm, with Bitcoin holding, ETH catching ETF flows, and geopolitical tension easing slightly. Some important shifts today around Russia, prediction markets, and MegaETH.

Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely. First real positive signal in weeks, even if Iran called it “buying time.” JD Vance cancelled his Pakistan trip. Hormuz remains unstable, with ships reportedly shelled after paying fake IRGC BTC and USDT tolls. Iran still threatens to break the blockade by force. Week eight, still unresolved.

US equities dropped over $400B in a day. NASDAQ at $24,310, down 0.6%. Gold fell 2.8% to $4,670. Silver at $76. Oil rising again. VIX at 19.5. US 10Y at 4.30%.

Fear & Greed flipped to Greed. Warsh pledged to divest $192M in crypto and venture exposure and said crypto is now part of the US financial system. That matters if he ends up running the Fed.

The bigger shift is Russia. A 327-0 vote passed a crypto legalization bill on first reading. Crypto will be treated as property and allowed in cross-border settlements. This is a clear move toward using crypto as a settlement layer outside SWIFT. That changes the geopolitical role of Bitcoin.

Bitcoin at $77,936, up 1%. Dominance 58.1%. Market cap $2.68T. IBIT bought $900M of BTC this week. Morgan Stanley ETF flows continue. Strategy is back in profit, Saylor still accumulating. The structural bid is there.

Ethereum at $2,348. ETH ETFs brought in $43M versus BTC ETFs at $11M. BlackRock is adding exposure. Staking crossed one third of supply.

Solana at $86. BNB at $636. XRP at $1.42 with ETF assets at $1.43B.

HYPE at $40.94, slightly up but under pressure as Polymarket and Kalshi launched perpetual futures. Direct competition before HIP-4 even goes live.

LINK at $9.20, steady.

Privacy cooled slightly after yesterday’s move. XMR at $364, ZEC at $315. STRK up another 14.4% to $0.048 after the Shinobi upgrade. Strong move, now needs real usage.

ARB at $0.125. Arbitrum froze 30,766 ETH linked to the Kelp hack. Lazarus is routing funds through DeFi, driving spikes in bridge activity. Total crypto hacks now exceed $17B.

PENGU at $0.0085, up 5.4%. NFT rotation continues. Yuga’s Grails OTC desk launches this week.

RAVE down another 37%. ZachXBT continues targeting supply-controlled tokens like Memecore. OKX backing investigations with a bounty shows a shift in how these markets are handled. Think the Rave saga is over. 

Prediction markets are expanding fast. Polymarket and Kalshi launching perps puts them in direct competition with Hyperliquid. At the same time, regulators are stepping in, with lawsuits targeting prediction market activity.

Stablecoins continue to scale. USDT supply hit $188B. Another $1B minted. Global adoption expanding through payments, trading, and new integrations.

Xeet moving from Abstract to MegaETH is a notable shift. Didn’t expect that. MegaETH pushes near real-time execution, which fits tournament-style use cases. But ecosystem depth is still limited compared to alternatives. The key is whether users actually follow.

For Abstract, this is a loss. Without a strong creator hub, the ecosystem weakens.

DeFi remains under pressure. Aave faces up to $341M in bad debt. Multiple protocols are still affected by the Kelp exploit. LayerZero’s default configuration exposed structural weaknesses. This will take time to resolve.

Anthropic dealing with access issues on Mythos. SpaceX and AI infrastructure plays continue to attract capital. New models, new agents, more competition across the board. AI is still accelerating.

FTX’s early bets are worth billions now. But it’s worth remembering how much value got destroyed in that forced liquidation cycle. 

Russia moving toward crypto settlement, ETH flows turning positive, STRK pushing privacy, and prediction markets expanding.

All while BTC is just grinding sideways.
